description: Placeholder description of what dropped in this patch. New nodes, balance changes, bug fixes, and whatever else made it in.
devNotes: Hello everyone! Welcome to update 0.8.0. This one was a big one for us — we've been working on the combat feel for a while and we're really happy with where it landed. Let us know what you think.

changes.text: New node: Reflect — bounces projectiles back at the sender
New node: Chain — links an ability to fire again after a delay
Dash now has a 0.3s window where it can cancel into an attack
Knockback scaling now factors in the target's current health
Fixed a bug where abilities could fire twice if triggered on the same frame
Removed the old Stun node — replaced by the new Condition system

description: The graph editor got a major overhaul. More node types, a cleaner UI, and the first pass at the condition system.
devNotes: This update took way longer than expected but I think it was worth it. The node editor is finally starting to feel like what I had in my head when I started this project.

changes.text: Condition nodes — abilities can now check health, distance, cooldown state, and more
8 new effect nodes across damage, movement, and utility categories
Node search bar in the editor
Graph editor UI rebuilt from scratch — cleaner, faster, easier to read
Node connections now show type colors to help avoid mismatches
Fixed graph not saving correctly when switching abilities mid-match

description: The first real public build. Core gameplay loop, basic node editor, and multiplayer battles.
devNotes: This is it. The first version anyone outside my friends list has played. It's rough around the edges but the core idea is here. Build an ability, fight someone, see what happens.

changes.text: Core graph editor with 12 starter nodes
